analyst_observations.skill_effectiveness: The korean-resume-builder skill significantly improves performance on structured, workflow-heavy tasks (eval-1 and eval-3). Both eval-1 and eval-3 show 40% improvement over baseline.
analyst_observations.assertion_patterns: Eval-2 is the 'easiest' (baseline passes at 83%), suggesting general knowledge about Jumpit-style resumes is available. Eval-1 and eval-3 show larger skill differentials, indicating the skill provides specialized value in structured workflows and decision-making frameworks.
key_discriminators: Workflow structure: Skill references explicit gather→choose→translate→fill→verify pattern; baseline provides general advice
Output organization: Skill recommends job-specific paths (output/{company}/{role}/); baseline misses this convention
Clarifying questions: Skill proactively asks user for preferences before proceeding; baseline makes recommendations instead
Verification commands: Skill provides specific pdfinfo and pdftoppm syntax; baseline mentions concepts without command details
analyst_observations.skill_readiness: The skill is working well across all three test cases. All with_skill runs achieve 100% pass rate. The skill's strength lies in structured workflows, clarity of next steps, and integration with career-ops conventions (output paths, config files, template assets).
